name: Entity wrappers retyped to Drizzle
outcome: The five entity wrapper classes implement the @jmondi OAuth interfaces over Drizzle row types instead of @prisma/client types.
scope: Retype the auth_code/client/scope/token/user entity classes to Drizzle $inferSelect; no repo or DB changes.
acceptance_criteria: Each entity class implements its @jmondi interface (OAuthClient/OAuthScope/OAuthToken/OAuthAuthCode/OAuthUser) using Drizzle row types
steps: src/app/oauth/entities/user.ts — retype constructor input from @prisma/client User to users.$inferSelect; preserve fields used by JWT/middleware (id, email, passwordHash)
src/app/oauth/entities/scope.ts — retype to oauthScopes.$inferSelect implementing OAuthScope
src/app/oauth/entities/client.ts — retype to oauthClients.$inferSelect with scopes; map allowedGrants/redirectUris to the OAuthClient interface
src/app/oauth/entities/auth_code.ts — retype to oauthAuthCodes.$inferSelect with client/user/scopes; keep isExpired and redirectUri mapping
src/app/oauth/entities/token.ts — retype to oauthTokens.$inferSelect with client/user/scopes; keep revoke() and expiry mapping
notes: Wrappers carry both the row shape and the @jmondi interface. Entity behavior (isExpired/revoke/scope mapping) is exercised by the repo and flow tests, so no standalone entity tests.
